clusters.title: U.S. Soldier Arrested for Betting on Maduro's Capture
clusters.summary: A U.S. soldier has been arrested for allegedly placing bets on the capture of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro through the online betting platform Polymarket, reportedly winning $400,000. The Justice Department has intervened, raising concerns about the legality and ethics of such wagers involving foreign political figures. This incident highlights the intersection of military personnel and online gambling in politically sensitive contexts.

items.title: Soldier arrested for alleged bet on Maduro's capture
items.description: Soldier arrested for alleged bet on Maduro's capture

items.title: US Soldier Arrested for Alleged Insider Betting on Maduro Capture
items.description: A U.S. special forces soldier, who was involved in the dramatic raid to capture Venezuela’s Nicolás Maduro, is under arrest and accused of using classified information about the raid to make prediction market bets, ultimately winning him more than $400,000. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY from the White House.

items.title: US soldier charged with using classified intel to win $400K Polymarket bet on Maduro raid - AP News
items.description: US soldier charged with using classified intel to win $400K Polymarket bet on Maduro raid  AP News

items.title: Soldier Used Classified Information to Bet on Maduro’s Ouster, U.S. Says
items.description: Federal prosecutors say that Sgt. Gannon Ken Van Dyke, who was involved in the operation to oust Nicolás Maduro from power in Venezuela, used the information to place bets on a prediction market.

items.title: US soldier involved in Maduro raid charged over alleged bets on capture
items.description:
Gannon Ken Van Dyke, who allegedly made more than $400,000 on Polymarket, could face up to 60 years in prison

A US soldier who played a role in the January capture of Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro is now in custody after allegedly cashing in over $400,000 on wagers about the politician’s removal from office, federal authorities announced on Thursday.

Prosecutors say beginning in early December the soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, was involved in planning for the military operation to capture and depose Maduro.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Israel-Hezbollah Ceasefire Extended by Three Weeks

The extension comes amid ongoing tensions, with accusations that Israel may be using the truce to further its military objectives. This development is significant as it seeks to stabilize the region while addressing the complexities of the conflict.

clusters.title: US Considers Suspension of Spain from NATO Over Iran War Support
clusters.summary: An internal Pentagon email indicates that the United States is contemplating suspending Spain from NATO due to frustrations over its support in the ongoing tensions related to the Iran war. NATO officials have clarified that there is no formal provision for expelling members, highlighting the complexities of alliance dynamics amidst international conflicts. This situation underscores the challenges faced by NATO as it navigates member states' varying levels of commitment to collective defense efforts.

clusters.title: Trump Orders Navy to Engage Iranian Boats in Hormuz Strait
clusters.summary: President Trump has directed the U.S. Navy to 'shoot and kill' any boats that are found laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, a vital maritime route for global oil shipments. This order comes amid heightened tensions with Iran, as the U.S. seeks to ensure the safety of navigation in the region. The directive underscores the escalating military posture in response to perceived threats from Iranian forces.

clusters.title: Trump's 'Gold Card' Visa Program Approves Only One Applicant
clusters.summary: The Trump administration's 'gold card' visa program, which requires a minimum investment of $1 million for foreigners to live and work in the U.S., has approved just one applicant since its launch in December. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick highlighted the limited uptake of the program, raising questions about its appeal and effectiveness in attracting foreign investment. This development underscores the challenges faced by the initiative in a competitive immigration landscape.

The situation raises concerns about maritime security and the potential for further confrontations.

clusters.title: Rep. David Scott of Georgia Dies at 80 While Seeking Reelection
clusters.summary: Representative David Scott, a Democrat from Georgia, has passed away at the age of 80 while campaigning for his 13th term in Congress. His death marks the fifth loss among members of the 119th Congress, raising questions about the future of his seat and the upcoming election. Scott was known for his long tenure and contributions to various legislative efforts.

clusters.title: DOJ Ends Criminal Investigation of Fed Chair Jerome Powell
clusters.summary: The Department of Justice has officially closed its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, concluding that there was insufficient evidence for further action. This decision may facilitate the confirmation of Kevin Warsh, a potential nominee for the Fed chair position, as the investigation had raised concerns about the leadership of the Federal Reserve. The outcome highlights the ongoing scrutiny of the Fed amid broader economic discussions.

clusters.title: Trump Threatens Tariffs on UK Over Digital Services Tax
clusters.summary: President Trump has warned that he may impose significant tariffs on the UK if it does not eliminate its digital services tax targeting U.S. technology companies. He criticized the UK for attempting to profit from American firms, raising concerns about the potential impact on the ongoing trade relationship between the two nations. This threat comes as discussions about a UK–US trade deal continue.

items.title: Trump says he will ‘probably put a big tariff on the UK’ if it doesn’t drop digital services tax
items.description:
President accuses Britain of trying to ‘make an easy buck’ from American tech firms, weeks after warning UK–US trade deal can be changed

  • Business live – latest updates

Donald Trump has threatened to impose “a big tariff” on the UK if it does not drop its digital services tax on US technology companies.

The digital services tax, introduced in 2020, imposes a 2% levy on the revenues of several big US tech giants.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Netanyahu Reveals Successful Treatment for Prostate Cancer
clusters.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced that he has successfully undergone treatment for early-stage prostate cancer, which was discovered during a routine check-up. He disclosed that he chose to keep his diagnosis private for two months to prevent potential exploitation by Iran amid ongoing tensions. Netanyahu stated that the treatment has left no trace of the cancer.

items.title: Netanyahu says he was successfully treated for prostate cancer
items.description:
Israeli prime minister says early-stage malignant tumour was discovered during a routine check-up

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

Benjamin Netanyahu has revealed that he received successful treatment for early-stage prostate cancer, without specifying when the treatment took place.

In a statement on social media, as his annual medical report was released, the Israeli prime minister said an early-stage malignant tumour had been discovered during a routine checkup. The 76-year-old said targeted treatment had removed “the problem” and left no trace of it.

Continue reading...

items.title: Netanyahu says he hid prostate cancer from public because of Iran war
items.description: The Israeli prime minister said he delayed revealing his diagnosis for two months to prevent Iran from using it as “propaganda.” He said treatment had left “no trace” of the cancer.

clusters.title: Rams Draft QB Ty Simpson Amid Subdued Reactions from Coaches
clusters.summary: The Los Angeles Rams selected quarterback Ty Simpson in the recent draft, although he has not met head coach Sean McVay or general manager Les Snead prior to the selection. Both McVay and Snead expressed a measured response to the draft choice, indicating a cautious approach as the team integrates Simpson into their plans. This decision highlights the Rams' ongoing efforts to bolster their roster amid a competitive landscape.

clusters.title: China Imposes Export Restrictions on EU Firms Over Taiwan Arms Sales
clusters.summary: China has announced a ban on the export of dual-use items to seven European entities in response to their involvement in arms sales to Taiwan. This marks the first time China has implemented such restrictions against European firms, highlighting escalating tensions over Taiwan's defense. The move is seen as a significant step in China's efforts to counter foreign military support for Taiwan.

clusters.summary: A man has been arrested for allegedly planning a mass shooting at a festival in New Orleans. clusters.title: Bank of England Official Warns of Overinflated Global Stock Markets
clusters.summary: A senior official from the Bank of England has cautioned that global stock markets are currently overvalued and likely to experience a decline. This statement marks a rare instance of a high-ranking bank figure openly discussing potential market downturns, highlighting concerns about economic stability. The warning raises questions about investor confidence and market resilience moving forward.

clusters.title: India Condemns Trump's 'Hellhole' Comment on Social Media
clusters.summary: India's Foreign Ministry has expressed strong disapproval of remarks made by US radio host Michael Savage, which were shared by former President Donald Trump, describing India as a 'hellhole.' The ministry labeled the comments as 'uninformed, inappropriate, and in poor taste,' highlighting the diplomatic sensitivities surrounding such characterizations.

items.title: India denounces ‘hellhole’ remark shared by Trump
items.description: India’s Foreign Ministry says comments by US radio host Michael Savage, circulated by Trump, are 'uninformed'.

items.title: India voices anger after Trump shares comments calling it a ‘hellhole’
items.description:
Foreign ministry says remarks of conservative podcast host Michael Savage that were shared by US president were ‘obviously uninformed, inappropriate and in poor taste’

The Indian government has denounced a social media post shared by Donald Trump which described India as a “hellhole”, calling the comments inappropriate and “in poor taste”.

On Wednesday, Trump posted a four page transcription of remarks made by conservative podcast host Michael Savage that denounced the US constitutional right to citizenship of everyone born in the country.

Continue reading...

clusters.summary: A judge has ordered the release of the family members of a suspect involved in a recent attack in Boulder, Colorado. clusters.title: US Allows Iran to Compete in 2026 World Cup Amid Restrictions
clusters.summary: The United States has confirmed that it does not object to the Iranian national soccer team participating in the 2026 World Cup. However, individuals with ties to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) will be barred from entering the country for the event. This decision highlights ongoing tensions between the US and Iran while allowing for the team's participation in a major international sporting event.

items.title: US says Iran can play at 2026 World Cup but bars those with ‘IRGC ties’
items.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the US has not told the Iranian national team that it cannot play.

clusters.summary: Cohere, a Canadian AI startup, has announced its acquisition of Aleph Alpha, a German AI company, as part of its strategy to enhance its presence in the European market. This move is seen as a significant step for Cohere to leverage Aleph Alpha's technology and expertise in AI development. The acquisition underscores the growing trend of cross-border investments in the AI sector.

The move highlights Taiwan's ongoing efforts to attract capital and strengthen its semiconductor industry amid global competition.

clusters.summary: Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro is preparing for the upcoming midterm elections, where his reelection is seen as crucial for maintaining Democratic influence in the swing state. The stakes are high as both parties mobilize their resources in this pivotal contest.

clusters.title: Utah Couple Charged in Child Kidnapping Case Involving Cuba
clusters.summary: A couple from Utah has been charged with parental kidnapping after allegedly taking their 10-year-old child to Cuba amidst a contentious custody dispute related to the child's gender identity. The child was returned to the U.S. by the Trump administration, highlighting the complexities surrounding custody cases involving gender issues. This incident raises significant legal and social questions about parental rights and child welfare.

clusters.title: Pentagon Addresses Timeline for Clearing Strait of Hormuz
clusters.summary: The Pentagon has provided a classified briefing to Congress regarding the timeline for clearing mines from the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting it could take up to six months. This assessment raises concerns about the potential impact on gasoline and oil prices, which may remain high through the upcoming midterm elections. The Pentagon has since dismissed the notion that such a lengthy process is definitive.
